title: Classic British Chicken Pie
description: A traditional British comfort food dish featuring a rich and creamy filling topped with a flaky pastry crust.
instructions: In a large pan, melt the butter over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the mushrooms to the pan and cook until they release their liquid and start to brown, about 5 minutes.
Add the chicken to the pan and cook until browned on all sides and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the flour to the pan and cook for 1-2 minutes.
Gradually add the milk, whisking continuously, and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Stir in the pie filling and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes.
Roll out the pie dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch. Place the dough over the filling and trim the edges to fit.
Crimp the edges of the pie crust to seal and cut a few slits in the top to allow steam to escape.
Brush the top of the pie crust with a little bit of milk and bake the pie in the preheated oven for 25-30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown.
Serve the pie with a side of mash.
